assessment_items.question_content:
**Nebuchadnezzar rebuilt the ancient city of $\_\_\_\_\_\_$ and this gate is part of that building campaign.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: **Nebuchadnezzar rebuilt the already ancient city of Babylon and this gate is part of that building campaign.**

assessment_items.question_content:
**The ancient city of Babylon was known for...**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: **The ancient city of Babylon was known for its gates, its ziggurat and its hanging gardens. **
**Dragons were associated with the god Marduk, who was the patron god of Babylon. **

choices.content: the god Marduk, who was the patron god of Babylon
the god Shamash, who was the god of justice and light
superstitions that would scare away foreign  invaders

assessment_items.question_content:
**The walls of the Ishtar Gate were made of . . .**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/3cca52fea9f71f27a2bfb3b7a1d03d82.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: **The bricks that make up the walls of the Ishtar Gate were made with the faience technique.**

choices.content: ordinary bricks clad with copper sheets
lapis lazuli and other semi-precious stones
